fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Maryland Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Maryland - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity maryland, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to maryland, speech-language pathologist compact maryland, maryland speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: Board of Examiners for Audiologists, Hearing Aid Dispensers, Speech-Language Pathologists, and Music Therapists

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in Maryland.

license_types.title: Provisional SLP License
license_types.description: Issued to SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.

steps.title: Complete Education and Clinical Fellowship
steps.description: Earn a master’s degree or doctorate in speech-language pathology and complete a supervised clinical fellowship.

steps.title: Pass the Praxis Examination
steps.description: Achieve a passing score on the Praxis Examination in Speech-Language Pathology.

steps.title: Apply for a Speech-Language Pathologist License
steps.description: Submit an application for licensure to the Maryland Board of Examiners for Audiologists, Hearing Aid Dispensers, Speech-Language Pathologists, and Music Therapists.

telepractice_text: Maryland permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.
provisional_text: Maryland issues a Provisional SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.
reciprocity_text: Maryland is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.

faqs.question: How much does a Maryland SLP license cost?
Is Maryland part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
Does Maryland allow telepractice for SLPs?
Does Maryland have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Maryland participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Maryland without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Maryland?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Maryland while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Maryland is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.

hero.title: Become a Speech-Language Pathologist in Maryland
hero.dek: Maryland Speech-Language Pathologist licenses require a master's degree, supervised professional experience, and passing the national exam.
